task_completion.description: The dashboard is named 'CDN Route Diagnostics'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the title 'CDN Route Diagnostics' at the top of the Graphite dashboard interface.

task_completion.description: A graph titled 'Edge Speed Comparison' is created plotting the two speed sensors.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a graph panel with the title 'Edge Speed Comparison'. The legend or metric configuration should show 'servers.web_traffic.speed_sensor_1' and 'servers.web_traffic.speed_sensor_2'.

task_completion.description: A graph titled 'Speed Divergence Delta' is created using diffSeries and absolute functions.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a graph panel titled 'Speed Divergence Delta'. The legend or metric configuration should show the use of 'absolute' and 'diffSeries' functions applied to the sensor metrics.

task_completion.description: A graph titled 'Aggregate Route Mean' is created using the averageSeries function.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a graph panel titled 'Aggregate Route Mean'. The legend or metric configuration should show the use of the 'averageSeries' function applied to the sensor metrics.

task_completion.description: The dashboard is saved and displays all three required graphs.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a screenshot showing the complete Graphite dashboard interface with all three graphs ('Edge Speed Comparison', 'Speed Divergence Delta', 'Aggregate Route Mean') visible simultaneously.

integrity.description: The agent used the Graphite web interface to create the dashboard.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for screenshots showing the Graphite web UI being actively used (e.g., clicking menus, adding graphs, configuring metrics) rather than terminal commands.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the GUI by writing the dashboard JSON directly in a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: Ensure there are no screenshots of a text editor (like nano, vim, or gedit) being used to manually write the dashboard JSON file.

integrity.description: The graphs render actual data lines.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for actual plotted lines on the graphs, indicating that the metrics are genuinely querying the Graphite backend and not just empty configurations.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The dashboard must be named exactly 'CDN Route Diagnostics'. It must contain exactly three graphs with the following exact titles: 'Edge Speed Comparison', 'Speed Divergence Delta', and 'Aggregate Route Mean'. The 'Speed Divergence Delta' graph must use diffSeries and absolute functions. The 'Aggregate Route Mean' graph must use the averageSeries function. The metrics used are servers.web_traffic.speed_sensor_1 and servers.web_traffic.speed_sensor_2.
